rulings.Case Title: IN RE APPLICATION OF: WARREN G. IRVING JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Response To Out Of State Subpoena; And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 3, 2019, Line 1, PETITIONER WARREN IRVING's Motion To Compel Response To Out Of State Subpoena; And Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. The Court reviewed the moving papers online, but requests the moving party to submit courtesy copies to the Court Clerk at the date and time of hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Rebecca.woodson@dentons.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MIDLAND FUNDING LLC VS. PHUONG TRAN
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 3, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MIDLAND FUNDING LLC's HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. Defendant Tran's claim of exemption is GRANTED. The levying officer is directed to release $200 to the judgment creditor for payment on the judgment and the remainder ($113.98 or anything else) to the judgment debtor. The court orders that the earnings withholding order now withholds $200/pay period ($400/month) from debtor's earnings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SAFEDOCS, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL VS. SWETT & CRAWFORD A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents From Defendant Sinclair Insurance Services
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 3, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFFs SAFEDOCS, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, TERRY BIBBER, SHIRLEY BIBBER, RUSS DAVIS, and KURT NEUMANN's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents From Defendant Sinclair Insurance Services Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff SAFEDOCS' Motion to Compel is GRANTED as to Requests for Production, Set Three, Request Nos. 1 and 7. The motion is DENIED as to Request Nos. 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9. The motion is under submission as to Request No. 10. The request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Rebecca.woodson@dentons.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MINOO PARSAZADEH VS. HAMPTON HEALTH, LTD. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, Order Of Contempt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 3, 2019, Line , PLAINTIFF MINOO PARSAZADEH's Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, Order Of Contempt Judges pro tem David McDonald and Paul Renne's well-reasoned reports and recommendations are adopted. Plaintiff Minoo Parsazadeh's motions for (1) terminating sanctions or, in the alternative, order of contempt and (2) protective order are DENIED. Plaintiff is to sit for her deposition on or before May 15, 2019. Plaintiff is to serve the at-issue discovery responses on or before May 13, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and attaches a copy of the discovery reports and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MOTIVA ENTERPRISES LLC A DELAWRE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. AXIS SURPLUS INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Axis And Endurance Notice Of Joint Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 3, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT ENDURANCE AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY and Defendants Axis'Joint Motion For Summary Judgment. Endurance's motion for summary judgment is OFF CALENDAR as moot because plaintiff filed an amended complaint. (See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Sup. Crt. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131.) Department 206 has given this department the discretion to continue the trial date. The trial date is continued to September 23, 2019 to allow defendants to re-new their motions for summary judgment/adjudication. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.=(302/RBU)
